mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to yield a positive return in >55% of cases over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects rising corporate output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher near-term earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day returns in >60% of observations during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth yields negative 60-day returns in >58% of monthly observations over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 0.5 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates severe economic stagnation that erodes corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below the threshold produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases during late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment rates signals a synchronized supply-side recovery and labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >55% of triggered months over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPI yields positive 60-day returns in >60% of observations during tightening regimes over a 3-year rolling window.
